title: Neural network modeling of psychoanalytic concepts.

assets.abstract: Techniques used over decades in brain-based neural network modeling are applied to understanding processes involved in psychoanalysis. Behavioral change is interpreted as a transition, using simulated annealing, from a less to a more optimal attractor in a competitive-cooperative dynamical system that includes analogs of the amygdala, prefrontal cortex, and hypothalamus, and the neurotransmitter norepinephrine. The article explores how psychoanalysis can facilitate the quest for the life that is

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Bridge paper score=6.0: links Wind/Pride concept to mechanistic neuroscience
